name: BOOK_TRIAGE
description: Pre-check routing operation for the BOOK workflow family. Classifies an input folder of written material, produces a BOOK_MANIFEST.json, and recommends which BOOK workflow to enter. Not a full swarm workflow — no worker spawning, no QA loops, no verdicts. A single-pass QUEEN-level classification. v1.1.0 extends v1.0.0 with per-chapter state classification (Step 3.5) when manifest scope is present. Backward-compatible: manifests without scope fall back to v1.0.0 aggregate-only behavior. Triggered by the phrase 'BOOK_TRIAGE'. Owner: Michael. Orchestrator: QUEEN (Claude in main conversation).

on_engage: QUEEN reads this JSON in full
QUEEN reports: 'BOOK_TRIAGE mode engaged. Ready. Point me to the source folder.'
QUEEN waits for explicit human direction specifying the folder path
trigger.source_folder_requires: explicit human direction specifying the directory containing source documents

relationship_to_book_family.contract: BOOK_TRIAGE produces BOOK_MANIFEST.json which all downstream BOOK workflows consume. TRIAGE determines entry point; human confirms.
relationship_to_book_family.handoff: On triage completion, human reviews BOOK_MANIFEST.json, fills template declarations, and triggers the recommended (or overridden) BOOK workflow.

step_1_inventory.name: INVENTORY
actions: list all files in the source folder recursively
count files by type (.md, .txt, .tex, .docx, .pdf, etc.)
note any existing structural files (TOC, README, chapter ordering, table of contents)
note any existing manifest or configuration files
produce file list with paths, sizes, and types

step_2_sampling.name: SAMPLING
actions: read the first ~100 lines and last ~50 lines of each text-based file

for each file, identify: file length (lines/words), presence of headers/structure, prose quality assessment, presence of TOC or chapter markers, presence of citations, presence of equations/figures/code, detected date/epoch if visible

step_3_per_file_classification.name: FILE_CLASSIFICATION

categories.fragment: short, unstructured, note-like, incomplete thoughts, research snippets
categories.draft: has structure (headers, sections) but prose is rough, incomplete, or contains TODO markers
categories.chapter: structured, substantial prose, appears to be a complete chapter or major section
categories.polished: structured, complete, prose is publication-quality or near it

actions: classify each file into one of the four categories
note confidence level for each classification (high/medium/low)
note any files that don't fit cleanly (e.g., a bibliography file, a glossary, an outline)

step_4_aggregate_assessment.name: AGGREGATE

quality_levels.rough: majority fragments and drafts, no coherent chapter structure → recommend BOOK_CONSOLIDATION
quality_levels.structured: majority chapters, some coherent ordering exists but no storyboard/arc → recommend BOOK_STORYBOARD
quality_levels.storyboarded: chapters exist with clear logical progression and pedagogical structure → recommend BOOK_EDITORIAL
quality_levels.polished: prose is complete, consistent, and high quality → recommend BOOK_PRODUCTION

actions: determine overall folder state based on file classifications
identify the recommended entry workflow
note any caveats (e.g., 'mostly structured but 3 files are fragments that need consolidation first')

step_5_genre_detection.name: GENRE_DETECTION
actions: attempt to identify genre from content (academic, exploratory, meta-study, hard sci-fi, math exposition, etc.)
attempt to identify voice patterns (Socratic, Feynman, authoritative, conversational, etc.)
attempt to identify target audience (bright students, peer researchers, general audience, etc.)
these are SUGGESTIONS — human fills in definitive values in manifest

step_3_5_per_chapter_classification.name: PER_CHAPTER_CLASSIFICATION
step_3_5_per_chapter_classification.condition: only runs if BOOK_MANIFEST.json already exists at project root with a 'scope' section containing 'intended_chapters'
step_3_5_per_chapter_classification.skip_condition: if no scope present in manifest — falls back to v1.0.0 aggregate-only behavior (step_4_aggregate_assessment output is the final classification)
actions: read scope.intended_chapters from existing BOOK_MANIFEST.json
for each intended chapter entry: search all source files for content matching chapter.target_topic
classify each intended chapter using CHAPTER_STATE_TAXONOMY.md vocabulary: MISSING | OUTLINE_ONLY | NOTES_ONLY | PARTIALLY_DRAFTED | DRAFT | CHAPTER | POLISHED
assign confidence level (high/medium/low) to each classification
record which source_files were associated with each chapter
produce triage.per_chapter_state array: [{ chapter_index, chapter_title, detected_state, confidence, source_files, notes }]
update scope.intended_chapters[].status with detected state for each chapter
note: if aggregate_assessment from step_4 is 'rough' and scope is present, recommend BOOK_COMPLETION as entry workflow (not BOOK_CONSOLIDATION)
outputs: possibly updated recommended_entry (BOOK_COMPLETION if mixed states detected)
step_3_5_per_chapter_classification.reference: workflows/BOOK/CHAPTER_STATE_TAXONOMY.md — state definitions and detection heuristics

step_6_produce_manifest.name: MANIFEST_GENERATION
actions: write BOOK_MANIFEST.json to project root
fill triage section completely (including per_chapter_state if step_3_5 ran)
fill templates section with best-guess suggestions or explicit placeholders
fill structure section with detected values or placeholders
fill production section with placeholders (human specifies)

step_7_report.name: REPORT
actions: present triage findings to human: file count, per-file classification table, aggregate assessment, recommended entry workflow, template suggestions, any caveats
instruct human to review and edit BOOK_MANIFEST.json before triggering next workflow
report engagement phrase for recommended workflow

manifest_schema.changelog: v1.1.0 adds scope section (intended_chapters, scope_declared_at, scope_revision_log) and per_chapter_state to triage section. Manifests without scope are still valid and fall back to v1.0.0 behavior.

fields.title: string — book title (human fills)
fields.author: string — author name (human fills)
fields.project_root: string — path to project root (TRIAGE fills)

triage.recommended_entry: BOOK_CONSOLIDATION | BOOK_STORYBOARD | BOOK_EDITORIAL | BOOK_PRODUCTION | BOOK_COMPLETION (TRIAGE fills; BOOK_COMPLETION recommended when scope present and mixed states detected)
triage.per_file_classifications: array of {file, category, confidence, notes} (TRIAGE fills)
triage.per_chapter_state: array of {chapter_index, chapter_title, detected_state, confidence, source_files, notes} (TRIAGE v1.1 fills when scope present; absent when no scope)

scope._note: OPTIONAL — v1.1.0 addition. If absent, TRIAGE behaves as v1.0.0. Schema documented in workflows/BOOK/BOOK_MANIFEST_SCOPE.md
scope.scope_declared_at: ISO timestamp (human fills when declaring scope)
scope.scope_revision_log: array of revision entries (append-only; empty array if scope never revised)
scope.intended_chapters: array of { index, title, slug, target_topic, target_length_words, rationale, status } (human fills; TRIAGE v1.1 fills status field)

templates.voice: string — VOICE_<name> if mode=composition (human fills, TRIAGE suggests)
templates.persona: string — PERSONA_<name> if mode=composition (human fills, TRIAGE suggests)
templates.style: string — STYLE_<name> if mode=composition (human fills, TRIAGE suggests)
templates.prose: string — PROSE_<name> if mode=composition (human fills, TRIAGE suggests)

structure.genre: string — genre classification (human fills, TRIAGE suggests)
structure.estimated_chapters: integer or null (TRIAGE estimates, human confirms)
structure.front_matter: array of strings — ordered list of front matter sections (human fills)
structure.back_matter: array of strings — ordered list of back matter sections (human fills)
structure.special_elements: array of strings — equations, figures, tables, code, proofs, etc. (TRIAGE detects, human confirms)

production.template_id: string — production template reference (human fills or deferred to BOOK_PRODUCTION)

notes.re_triage: There is no 're-triage' operation. If the human disagrees with the assessment, they edit BOOK_MANIFEST.json directly. TRIAGE is a one-shot classification.
notes.template_resolution: TRIAGE may suggest templates if it recognizes voice/style patterns in the source material, but template declarations are ultimately human-owned. TRIAGE suggestions go in the manifest as placeholders that the human confirms or overrides.
notes.entry_flexibility: The human can override TRIAGE's recommended entry workflow. A folder TRIAGE classifies as 'rough' can be entered at BOOK_EDITORIAL if the human knows the material is actually polished — TRIAGE recommends, human decides.
notes.v1_1_scope_note: When scope is present (v1.1.0 behavior), TRIAGE recommends BOOK_COMPLETION if it detects mixed states (some chapters present, some absent). The human may override. BOOK_COMPLETION requires the scope section to function — do not trigger BOOK_COMPLETION without it.
notes.per_chapter_state_note: The per_chapter_state array in the triage section is TRIAGE v1.1's primary new output. It is consumed by BOOK_COMPLETION. If TRIAGE is re-run (e.g., after the human adds more source files), per_chapter_state is updated in place.
notes.taxonomy_reference: State vocabulary for per_chapter_state classification: workflows/BOOK/CHAPTER_STATE_TAXONOMY.md
